fragment:
<set-variable name="metadata-config" value="@{return @"{
        'models': {
            //{modelsConfigCode}
        },
        'api-types': {
            'responses-v1': {
                'base-path': '/openai/v1/responses',
                'path-segment': '/openai/v1/responses',
                'api-version': 'v1'
            },
            'openai-v1': {
                'base-path': '/openai/v1',
                'path-segment': '/deployments',
                'api-version': 'v1'
            },
            'responses': {
                'base-path': '/openai/responses',
                'path-segment': '/responses',
                'api-version': '2025-03-01-preview'
            },
            'geminiopenai': {
                'base-path': '/v1beta/openai',
                'path-segment': '/v1beta/openai',
                'api-version': 'v1beta',
                'compatible-pool-types': 'gemini-openai'
            },
            'openai': {
                'base-path': '/openai',
                'path-segment': '/deployments',
                'api-version': '2024-02-15-preview'
            },
            'inference': {
                'base-path': '/models',
                'path-segment': '/models',
                'api-version': '2024-05-01-preview',
                // Universal-LLM-style inference endpoint. Allow OpenAI-compatible pools
                // (Foundry inference, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock Mantle, Gemini OpenAI-compat).
                // Per-provider URL rewriting is handled by path-builder via
                // backend-path-templates[poolType][operation] when poolType is one of the
                // non-Foundry providers; ai-foundry/azure-openai keep the legacy
                // {base-path}/chat/completions shape.
                'compatible-pool-types': 'ai-foundry,azure-openai,aws-bedrock-mantle,gemini-openai'
            },
            // Native API access via // prefix. Each one strips its own prefix and
            // forwards the remainder of the path unchanged to the backend, then filters
            // pool selection by 'compatible-pool-types' so only the matching native pool
            // (e.g. aws-bedrock for /bedrock/, gemini for /gemini/, anthropic for /claude/)
            // can serve the request even if other pools (e.g. aws-bedrock-mantle) advertise
            // the same model name.
            'bedrock-native': {
                'base-path': '/bedrock',
                'path-segment': '/model',
                'api-version': 'bedrock-2024-04-15',
                'compatible-pool-types': 'aws-bedrock'
            },
            'gemini-native': {
                'base-path': '/gemini',
                'path-segment': '/models',
                'api-version': 'v1beta',
                'compatible-pool-types': 'gemini'
            },
            'claude-native': {
                'base-path': '/claude',
                'path-segment': '/v1/messages',
                'api-version': 'anthropic-2023-06-01',
                'compatible-pool-types': 'anthropic'
            },
            'openai-compat': {
                'base-path': '/v1',
                'path-segment': '',
                'api-version': 'v1',
                'compatible-pool-types': 'ai-foundry,azure-openai,aws-bedrock-mantle,gemini-openai'
            },
            // OpenAI-style images surface (/v1/images/generations, /v1/images/edits). The model
            // is taken from the JSON body (generations) or the `x-ai-model` header (multipart
            // edits). Longest base-path match ('/v1/images' > '/v1') guarantees image requests
            // never collide with the chat-oriented 'openai-compat' api-type. Only image-capable
            // pool types are allowed; the gpt-image family rides ai-foundry/azure-openai, while
            // FLUX and MAI use the dedicated azure-flux / azure-mai pool types.
            'image': {
                'base-path': '/v1/images',
                'path-segment': '',
                'api-version': 'v1',
                'compatible-pool-types': 'ai-foundry,azure-openai,azure-flux,azure-mai'
            }
        },
        'model-aliases': {
            //{modelAliasesCode}
        },
        'backend-path-templates': {
            // ai-foundry / azure-openai use the v1 OpenAI-compatible endpoint family
            // (`/openai/v1/...`). This endpoint accepts `api-version=v1` (which is
            // what the `inference` and `openai-compat` api-types set), reads the
            // model from the request body, and avoids the AOAI deployments path
            // (`/openai/deployments/{model}/...`) that requires a real preview
            // api-version (e.g. `2024-12-01-preview`). Keep `/openai/responses`
            // for the Responses API which retains its own URL contract.
            'ai-foundry': {
                'chat/completions': '/openai/v1/chat/completions',
                'embeddings': '/openai/v1/embeddings',
                'responses': '/openai/v1/responses',
                'images/generations': '/openai/v1/images/generations',
                'images/edits': '/openai/v1/images/edits',
                'models': '/openai/v1/models'
            },
            'azure-openai': {
                'chat/completions': '/openai/v1/chat/completions',
                'embeddings': '/openai/v1/embeddings',
                'responses': '/openai/v1/responses',
                'images/generations': '/openai/v1/images/generations',
                'images/edits': '/openai/v1/images/edits'
            },
            'aws-bedrock-mantle': {
                'chat/completions': '/v1/chat/completions',
                'responses': '/v1/responses',
                'models': '/v1/models'
            },
            'gemini-openai': {
                'chat/completions': '/v1beta/openai/chat/completions'
            },
            'aws-bedrock': {
                'chat/completions': '/model/{model}/converse'
            },
            'anthropic': {
                'chat/completions': '/v1/messages',
                'messages': '/v1/messages'
            },
            // Black Forest Labs FLUX image models (native BFL surface). The {modelPath}
            // placeholder is the BFL model slug (e.g. flux-2-pro) resolved from the model's
            // `modelPath` metadata — it differs from the model name (FLUX.2-pro) and is set
            // once at onboarding. path-builder injects api-version=preview for this pool type.
            'azure-flux': {
                'images/generations': '/providers/blackforestlabs/v1/{modelPath}',
                'images/edits': '/providers/blackforestlabs/v1/{modelPath}'
            },
            // Microsoft MAI image models (native MAI surface). The model travels in the request
            // body; no api-version query parameter is required.
            'azure-mai': {
                'images/generations': '/mai/v1/images/generations',
                'images/edits': '/mai/v1/images/edits'
            }
        },
        'cache-settings': {
            'config-version': '1.0.0',
            'ttl-seconds': 300
        },
        'timeout-settings': {
            'streaming-multiplier': 3
        }
    }";}" />
